methods.name: Add()
methods.summary: Adds a new material to the table based on the default material.

returns.doc: The position of the new material in the table.

methods.name: Add(Material)
methods.summary: Adds a new material to the table based on a given material.

params.name: material
params.doc: A model of the material to be added.

returns.doc: The position of the new material in the table.

methods.name: Add(Material, Boolean)
methods.signature: public int Add(Material material, bool reference)
methods.summary: Adds a new material to the table based on a given material.

params.name: material
params.doc: A model of the material to be added.

params.name: reference
params.doc: true if this material is supposed to be a reference material. Reference materials are not saved in the file.

returns.doc: The position of the new material in the table.

methods.name: DeleteAt
methods.summary: Removes a material at a specific position from this material table.

returns.doc: true if successful. false if materialIndex is out of range or the material cannot be deleted because it is the current material or because it material contains active geometry.

methods.name: Find(Guid, Boolean)
methods.signature: public int Find(Guid materialId, bool ignoreDeletedMaterials)
methods.summary: Finds a material with a matching id.

params.name: materialId
params.doc: A material ID to be found.

params.name: ignoreDeletedMaterials
params.doc: If true, deleted materials are not checked.

returns.doc: >=0 index of the material with the given name -1 no material has the given name.

methods.name: Find(String, Boolean)
methods.signature: public int Find(string materialName, bool ignoreDeletedMaterials)
methods.summary: Finds a material with a given name.

params.name: materialName
params.doc: Name of the material to search for. The search ignores case.

params.name: ignoreDeletedMaterials
params.doc: true means don't search deleted materials.

returns.doc: >=0 index of the material with the given name -1 no material has the given name.

methods.name: FindId
methods.summary: Uses the guid to find a model component. Deleted objects cannot be found by id. The guid is the value that is stored in the .Id property. In a single document, no two active objects have the same guid. If an object is replaced with a new object, then the guid persists. For example, if the _Move command moves an object, then the moved object inherits its guid from the starting object. If the Copy command copies an object, then the copy gets a new guid. This guid persists through file saving/opening operations. This function will not find grip objects.

params.name: id
params.doc: ID of model component to search for.

returns.doc: Reference to the rhino object with the objectId or null if no such object could be found.

methods.name: FindIndex
methods.summary: Retrieves a Material object based on Index. This search type of search is discouraged. We are moving towards using only IDs for all tables.

returns.doc: A Material object, or null if none was found.

methods.name: GetEnumerator
methods.summary: Returns the enumerator that yields all items.

methods.name: Modify
methods.signature: public bool Modify(Material newSettings, int materialIndex, bool quiet)

params.name: materialIndex
params.doc: zero based index of material to set. This must be in the range 0 <= layerIndex < MaterialTable.Count.

params.name: quiet
params.doc: if true, information message boxes pop up when illegal changes are attempted.

returns.doc: true if successful. false if materialIndex is out of range or the settings attempt to lock or hide the current material.

properties.name: Count
properties.summary: Returns the count of all items, including deleted ones.

properties.name: CurrentMaterialIndex
properties.summary: At all times, there is a "current" material. Unless otherwise specified, new objects are assigned to the current material. The current material is never locked, hidden, or deleted.

properties.name: CurrentMaterialSource
properties.summary: Gets or sets the current material source.

properties.name: Item
properties.summary: Conceptually, the material table is an array of materials. The operator[] can be used to get individual materials. A material is either active or deleted and this state is reported by Material.IsDeleted.
